# Writing Brief — LOCALE

*Generated by /brief on 2026-03-26*

## Venue & Constraints
- Target: NeurIPS 2026 (project final report format)
- Page limit: 6 pages body + appendix
- Format: neurips_2026.sty (LaTeX)
- Existing draft: main.tex exists but references XN-030 (pre-bug-fix); needs update to XN-031/XN-035

## Core Contribution (1 sentence)

LOCALE discovers that 97.9% of CI-test errors in PC skeletons are false colliders (NCO), and exploits this via ego-graph batched LLM orientation with non-collider-only constraints, achieving significant F1 gains on 3/10 benchmarks with 2-4x fewer queries and superior context efficiency over per-edge methods.

## Key Findings

1. **NCO dominance**: 97.9% of CI errors across 6 networks and 7 sample sizes are false colliders (XN-022). Method-agnostic, immediately adoptable by any CI-based pipeline.
2. **10-network comparison**: LOCALE 7W/2T/1L vs MosaCD across all MosaCD benchmarks (XN-035). Mean delta +6.7pp. Wilcoxon p=0.055.
3. **Core 5-network (12 seeds, Holm-corrected)**: Sachs +30.7pp (p<0.0001, d=5.61), Insurance +8.8pp (p=0.0125, d=1.04), Alarm +3.9pp (ns), Child +1.1pp (ns), Asia -6.7pp (SIG LOSS) — XN-031.
4. **Hepar2 (4 seeds)**: +16.0pp (p=0.013, d=3.08). 3rd significant win — XN-034.
5. **Context efficiency**: MosaCD breaks at 2048 tokens (Insurance -28.1pp). LOCALE robust at 2048 (0.853±0.017) — XN-029.
6. **Query savings**: ego-graph batching uses 2-4x fewer LLM queries across all networks.
7. **Data seed bug self-discovered and corrected**: headline robust (D-A02, XN-031 supersedes XN-030).

## Mechanism Story

PC-stable skeletons contain systematic CI errors. LOCALE discovers these are overwhelmingly false colliders (NCO finding), not false non-colliders. By enforcing only non-collider constraints in Max-2SAT, LOCALE avoids the damage that collider constraints cause. Ego-graph prompting gives the LLM richer local context (neighborhood structure) than per-edge queries, enabling joint orientation of all incident edges. The combination — NCO constraints + ego-graph batching — produces better accuracy with fewer queries. The tradeoff: degree-1 nodes get single-endpoint coverage, causing systematic errors on small networks with leaf nodes (Asia).

## Anticipated Reviewer Objections

| # | Objection | From | How to Address | Where in Paper |
|---|-----------|------|---------------|----------------|
| 1 | MosaCD re-implementation fidelity: Alarm 80.1% vs published 93% (12.9pp gap). Comparing against degraded baseline? | RN-001 W1, RN-002 | Acknowledge model gap (Qwen-27B vs GPT-4o-mini-128K). Position as same-model comparison isolating method difference. Note published MosaCD uses 128K context + 20K samples vs our 4096 + 10K. | §Experiments, Limitations |
| 2 | 3/5 proposal hypotheses unsupported (H3 DS failed, H4 Meek no-op, H5 untested). Method simpler than proposed. | RN-001 W2, RN-002 | Reframe: negative results are informative. DS too sparse, Meek unnecessary when Max-2SAT orients all. Simplification is a finding, not a weakness. | §Method, §Discussion |
| 3 | No synthetic experiments (proposal §5.5 untouched). All benchmarks are small BNLearn networks possibly in LLM training data. | RN-001 W3, RN-002 | Disguised robustness (XN-033) partially controls for memorization. Acknowledge as limitation. Structural constraints (NCO) work regardless of naming. | §Limitations |
| 4 | Asia loss is structural (degree-1 vulnerability). Any network with leaf nodes is affected. | RN-001 W5 | Quantified in XN-032. Degree-1 gap is -25pp on Asia, -17pp on Alarm, but +5-7pp on Insurance/Child. Network-dependent, not universal. Price of query efficiency — acknowledged. | §Analysis, Limitations |
| 5 | Single model family (Qwen3.5-27B only). No GPT-4, Claude, Llama. | RN-001 W7, RN-002 | Acknowledge. 9B ablation (XN-023) shows scale matters. Cross-model is future work. | §Limitations |
| 6 | Only Sachs survives Holm correction across 10 tests. Aggregate significance weak (Wilcoxon p=0.055). | XN-035 | Report both: individual Holm-corrected tests AND aggregate pattern (7W/2T/1L, Wilcoxon p=0.055). Pattern is consistent even if individual tests lack power. | §Results |
| 7 | Alpha=0.10 for Asia is post-hoc tuning. Cherry-picking? | RN-002 W9 | Report both alpha=0.05 and 0.10 results. Alpha sensitivity is a finding, not tuning — shows skeleton quality matters. | §Appendix |
| 8 | Contribution list inflated: F1 decomposition is obvious, safety valve is standard engineering. | RN-002 W6 | Trim to 2-3 core contributions: NCO discovery, ego-graph batching + query efficiency, context efficiency. | §Introduction |
| 9 | Published MosaCD gap unexplained. Model confound remains. | RN-001, RN-002 | Cannot fully resolve without running GPT-4o-mini. Transparent reporting: same-model comparison is fair within its scope. Published comparison in separate column. | §Results table |

## Dead Ends (mention in paper?)

| What | Why Abandoned | Paper Treatment |
|------|--------------|-----------------|
| LLM skeleton refinement (I10) | 0 TP across 6 networks. Reachability limit + LLM conservatism. | Mention as negative result strengthening F1 decomposition |
| Iterative BFS decimation (E02) | Single-pass K=10 beats multi-round. Error amplification. | Omit (engineering detail) |
| Dawid-Skene reconciliation (H3) | Too sparse: 2 annotators per edge. -11.6pp on Insurance. | Mention: proposed, tested, rejected. Simplification is a finding. |
| Meek propagation (H4) | Max-2SAT orients everything. Meek has nothing to do. | Brief mention: propagation unnecessary when local solver is complete. |
| Phase 6 calibration (H5) | Descoped — needs held-out graph instances. | Acknowledge in Limitations as future work. |

## Limitations to Acknowledge

- Single model family (Qwen3.5-27B). Ego-graph joint reasoning may be emergent at 27B+ scale.
- No synthetic experiments. All benchmarks are small BNLearn networks (5-76 nodes).
- MosaCD re-implementation uses different model and context window than published version. Same-model comparison is fair, but absolute numbers may differ.
- Asia loss structural: degree-1 nodes get single-endpoint coverage. Affects any network with leaf nodes.
- Stability is network-dependent, not universal. LOCALE more stable on Insurance/Sachs, less on Asia/Alarm.
- 3/5 proposal hypotheses unsupported or untested (H3-H5). Method is simpler than proposed.
- Alpha=0.10 for Asia is post-hoc. Skeleton quality sensitivity not systematically explored.
- No latent variable handling. Assumes causal sufficiency.
- Disguised robustness shows domain knowledge helps on some networks (Insurance, Sachs) and hurts on others (Alarm). Not domain-agnostic.

## Proposal Drift

| Planned | Actual | Justified? |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| 7-phase pipeline (Phases 0-6) | 3 active phases (skeleton + ego-scoring + NCO Max-2SAT + confidence vote) | Yes — DS, Meek, fallback, calibration all tested and rejected/descoped with evidence |
| H3: DS reconciliation improves over majority vote | DS rejected (-11.6pp). Confidence-weighted majority vote used. | Yes — structural mismatch (2 annotators too sparse) |
| H4: Meek propagation resolves easy edges | Meek is a no-op (Max-2SAT orients everything) | Yes — design finding |
| H5: Calibration + abstention | Not built (Phase 6 descoped) | Partial — promising preliminary results (XN-020) but needs held-out instances |
| Synthetic experiments (§5.5) | Not done | No — gap. Must acknowledge. |
| Cross-model validation | Only 27B + 9B ablation | Partial — 9B shows scale matters, but no GPT-4/Claude |
| Primary claim: orientation-layer | Shifted to NCO discovery + ego-graph as vehicle | Yes — data-driven reframe (A01) |

## Missing Pieces

- **Synthetic experiments**: 100-node ER/scale-free DAGs absent. Proposal §5.5 untouched.
- **Cross-model comparison**: No GPT-4, Claude, Llama experiments.
- **Hailfinder network**: 56 nodes, missing from 10-network comparison.
- **EBCC sensitivity analysis**: Dependence-aware reconciliation not tested (DS already rejected, but EBCC was mandatory per proposal).
- **Published MosaCD fidelity validation**: Alarm gap (12.9pp vs published) unexplained.
- **Bibliography**: All 18 bib entries in references.bib are PLACEHOLDER stubs. Human must verify.
- **Figures**: No figures in paper draft. Pipeline diagram, NCO chart, context sensitivity plot needed.
- **NeurIPS checklist**: All TODOs unfilled.

## New Literature to Position Against

| Paper | Relevance | How to Handle |
|-------|-----------|---------------|
| SNOE (LN-005) | Same abstract problem (edge orientation in CPDAGs), statistical tests, has theoretical guarantees LOCALE lacks | Related work: acknowledge as non-LLM baseline with guarantees. LOCALE is complementary (uses LLM priors). |
| BFS prompting (Susanti & Farber, LN-006) | Independently validates ego-graph superiority for LLM causal reasoning | Related work: supporting evidence for ego-graph design choice. |
| CauScientist (LN-008) | New 2026 competitor, multi-agent causal discovery | Related work: different operating point (full graph, multi-agent). Include in comparison table. |
| MosaCD confirmed ICLR 2026 (LN-008) | Primary baseline is now published at top venue | Strengthens our comparison choice. Cite as ICLR 2026. |
